scope: Port mid-turn request_user_input and MCP elicitation request/response handling into the AgenC runtime.

requiredBehaviors: Expose question, option, answer, response, and event shapes with call id, turn id, questions, and answer maps.
Publish request_user_input with the donor schema: questions array, required id/header/question, optional fill-text options, and required option label/description when options are present.
Enforce one to three questions and either zero fill-text options or two to three choice options per question before emitting a UI prompt.
Reject secret questions because the TUI prompt path does not provide input masking or transcript redaction.
Mark request_user_input as interactive, non-parallel, concurrency-unsafe, and not recovery replay-safe.
Describe availability in Plan mode by default and Default or Plan mode when the staged feature is enabled.
edgeCases: Zero or four questions are rejected before session state is touched.
One or four options are rejected before session state is touched, while omitted or empty options create fill-text questions.
Questions with isSecret=true are rejected before session state is touched.
Every accepted question is normalized to include the UI-added Other option.
Subagent sessions are rejected before prompting.

requiredBehaviors: Store pending user-input responders in active turn state keyed by the unique request id/call id.
Emit request_user_input with request id, call id, turn id, and normalized questions.
Resolve and remove the pending responder through notifyUserInputResponse.
Clean up stale pending user input on abort or active-turn cleanup.
edgeCases: No active turn returns a cancellation-style null response.
Multiple request_user_input prompts in the same turn do not overwrite each other.
External, turn-local, or session abort signals clean up the pending map.

requiredBehaviors: Expose a daemon-visible elicitation.respond method that accepts session id, request id, kind, optional MCP server name, and response payload.
Route daemon responses through the active background agent into the live session notify methods.
Forward session elicitation waits as typed daemon notifications and bridge attached TUI resolver answers back through elicitation.respond.
Expose TUI daemon-session helpers for request_user_input and MCP elicitation responses.
Install a production TUI prompt bridge that displays pending user-input and MCP elicitation events and resolves them through normal prompt submission.
Direct TUI sessions delegate request_user_input and MCP elicitation waits through installed resolver hooks.
Daemon-backed TUI sessions fan out daemon events to multiple subscribers while running resolver side effects exactly once per event.
Direct TUI resolver hooks receive abort signals so stale prompts are removed when their owning session or turn is cancelled.
Queue overlapping TUI resolver requests FIFO so a later request cannot replace an unresolved active request.
Expose accept, decline, and cancel actions for MCP TUI prompts.
Validate MCP form prompt input against primitive schema constraints, including titled string and multi-select enum variants, before accepting values.
Route composer submissions through the active elicitation prompt before falling back to normal session submit.
Validate malformed response payloads before touching session pending maps.
edgeCases: MCP responses require serverName.
request_user_input answers must be arrays of strings.
Two TUI resolver requests that arrive before the first submit resolve in arrival order.
Transcript and MCP URL completion subscribers can be active together without duplicate prompts or duplicate daemon responses.
Aborted direct TUI user-input and MCP prompts are removed from the active queue and resolve as cancellation.
URL and form MCP prompts can be accepted, declined, or cancelled from the TUI prompt.
Invalid boolean, integer, string enum, titled string enum, array enum, and titled multi-select MCP form entries keep the active prompt pending.
Composer submit cleanup still runs after either elicitation handling or normal session submit.
Unknown or already-resolved pending requests return an invalid request through the daemon lifecycle layer.

requiredBehaviors: Advertise form and URL elicitation capabilities when session handlers are attached without claiming form default application.
Register SDK request and completion handlers before MCP connect.
Restore request context metadata while stripping progressToken.
Serialize response metadata as protocol _meta.
Reject malformed URL and form request params instead of coercing missing required fields into empty prompts.
Normalize SDK-compatible titled enum schemas for string oneOf and array items.anyOf form fields without losing their const values.
Only auto-accept empty form elicitations when granular MCP elicitations are explicitly allowed.
Decline requests rejected by the approval policy.
edgeCases: URL elicitations use elicitationId as the pending key so completion notifications can resolve them.
Direct TUI URL elicitations resolve through matching completion notifications.
Daemon-backed TUI URL completion notifications clear the local prompt without sending a duplicate elicitation response.
Explicit daemon-backed TUI URL submissions still send exactly one elicitation response.
Missing URL, elicitation id, message, requestedSchema.properties, primitive schema type, titled enum const value, or array item enum definition is rejected.
on_request empty forms still surface to the session instead of auto-accepting.
MCP client capability advertisement stays aligned with the local parser and does not promise default application.
Granular MCP elicitation config denies or auto-accepts empty forms according to mcp_elicitations.
Unknown or missing metadata does not crash otherwise valid normalization.
Granular policy rejects unless a granular config explicitly allows MCP elicitations.

requiredBehaviors: Store pending MCP elicitation responders under active turn state by server and request id.
Emit mcp_elicitation_request and mcp_elicitation_complete events for UI surfaces.
Toggle outOfBandElicitationPaused while a request is pending.
Resolve and remove pending responders through notifyMcpElicitationResponse.
edgeCases: No active turn or abort returns a cancel-style null response.
Direct TUI MCP resolver aborts remove the visible prompt and release the session pause.
Multiple overlapping out-of-band elicitations keep the pause subject true until all finish.
